label: 525_small states_natural disasters_disasters_subject climate
description: Small countries face greater risks from natural disasters and climate change. They also struggled more during the COVID-19 pandemic, highlighting their unique challenges in global markets.

central_articles.excerpt: Versions in 中文 (Chinese), and Français (French) Small states are far more vulnerable than other countries to natural disasters and climate change. central_articles.title: Small states are the canary in the coal mine for the global economy, and they are struggling
central_articles.excerpt: Small states—countries with a population of 1.5 million or less—were hit particularly hard by the COVID-19 pandemic. They’ve also had a much slower recovery than other emerging market and developing economies (EMDEs). Small states have several features that make them especially vulnerable to global economic and…
